rawContent:
Unfortunately, I'm unable to access live road condition data from the WSDOT website. The WSDOT Travel Center pages require JavaScript to load dynamic content, which cannot be retrieved through standard web fetching tools.

**What I found:**
- The WSDOT website (wsdot.wa.gov) has a Travel Center with real-time data including mountain passes, travel alerts, cameras, and weather conditions, but it requires an interactive JavaScript-enabled browser to access
- The National Weather Service Seattle office reports some local flooding affecting rivers and roads, advising drivers to never drive around barricades

**To get current road conditions for your requested routes, I recommend:**

1. **Visit WSDOT Travel Center directly:** https://wsdot.wa.gov/travel/real-time/map (interactive map with all conditions)
2. **Call 511** (Washington State travel info hotline)
3. **Check mountain pass reports:** https://wsdot.com/travel/real-time/mountainpasses
4. **Download the WSDOT mobile app** for real-time alerts

These sources will provide current conditions for I-5, I-90 (Snoqualmie Pass), Highway 20 (North Cascades), and SR-20 to Anacortes including closures, delays, construction, and weather advisories.
content:
**ROAD CONDITIONS SUMMARY**

**I-5:** Status unavailable - real-time data inaccessible

**I-90 (Snoqualmie Pass):** Status unavailable - check mountain pass reports

**Highway 20 (North Cascades):** Status unavailable - real-time data inaccessible

**SR-20 to Anacortes:** Status unavailable - real-time data inaccessible

**General Advisory:** ⚠️ Caution - Local flooding reported by National Weather Service affecting rivers and roads. Never drive around barricades.

**Access Current Conditions:**
- WSDOT Travel Center: wsdot.wa.gov/travel/real-time/map
- Call 511 for Washington State travel info
- Mountain passes: wsdot.com/travel/real-time/mountainpasses
- WSDOT mobile app for real-time alerts

*Note: Live road condition data requires JavaScript-enabled browser access to WSDOT systems.*
